title: asperula hexaphylla subsp. capitata
intro: Asperula hexaphylla subsp. capitata, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant in the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and fragrant leaves. Traditionally, Woodruff has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Woodruff include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, as well as its use as a natural antacid and diuretic. Woodruff is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various conditions, including stomach issues and skin problems.
